document.title: M1 Runtime API Outline
document.purpose: Outline the intended programmer-facing runtime model and API for working with loaded M1 documents, overlay edits, cache selection, sourcing, ordering, loading, and saving.

scope.description: This document describes the in-memory runtime model and the API intended for application programmers. It is not the core M1 ontology spec and not the transport spec.
notes: This API is distinct from the browser.
This API is intended for programs that load, inspect, edit, prioritize, and re-emit M1 data.

uuid.note: If earlier conversation says GUID, interpret it as UUID.

aspect_id.description: Identifier of an aspect. In line with the M1 core/transport model, this may be a Tag URI or another entity-id form.

design_principles: All loaded claims are preserved by preserving all loaded M1 documents in memory.
The runtime also provides a selected current value per entity_id + aspect_id.
Overlay is a writable session layer consulted before priority.
Priority governs document search order after overlay.
None is a legitimate covering aspect value.
The conventional entity tombstone aspect is a covering value for an entire entity.
Document UUID, not file hash, is the normative identity of an M1 document in this runtime model.
An M1 transport UUID identifies a specific published transport artifact and must not be reused for changed content.
Hashes may still be useful for deduplication.

overlay.description: Session contributions / edits to M1 state.
rules: Overlay is checked before priority.
Overlay is not part of the priority list.
When written, overlay must have m1.id, m1.timestamp, and entities, even if entities is {}.
m1.id is bound to the specific emitted transport artifact and must not be reused for changed content.
m1.series_id is optional and identifies a persistent document-series lineage.
m1.created is optional and indicates when the document series was created.
Every time an .m1 file is output, m1.timestamp is updated to the immediate current timestamp of output.
Every time an .m1 file is output, it must receive a fresh UUID in m1.id.
Table remains a valid and important part of M1 transport, but runtime handling of table is deferred.
When set_aspect(...) writes into overlay, overlay m1.id is nullified.
set_uuid(uuid) manually specifies overlay.m1.id.
Aspect values in overlay may be None and serialize as JSON null.

overlay_headers.description: User-specified header values to apply when writing overlay.
rules: overlay_headers starts as an empty dictionary.
Header helper functions write into overlay_headers.
The intended UUID for the next overlay emission is tracked in overlay_headers['id'].
On amend save, overlay_headers is merged on top of the existing on-disk m1 header.
If a header key is not present in overlay_headers, it does not erase an existing header value.
Runtime-controlled emission fields still receive fresh output values: m1.id and m1.timestamp.
overlay_table is separate from entity/aspect overlay content.
overlay_table is only loaded or saved when the t flag is present.
Table identifiers follow the transport spec: UUID or Tag URI.

cache.description: Selected visible aspect value per entity_id + aspect_id.
rules: Cache is a selected view, not the full preservation layer.
Cache is derived from overlay plus priority.
table_cache works similarly to the entity/aspect cache.
overlay_table is consulted before priority.
Then loaded table contributions are consulted in priority order.
When an M1 document with table entries is loaded, table_cache for those identifiers is invalidated rather than eagerly recomputed.

source.description: Selected source document per cached aspect value.
rules: Source is a selected view paired with cache.
Source is derived from overlay plus priority.
This is the primary claim-preserving layer.
Keeping all loaded M1 documents preserves all loaded competing claims.

M1_loaded_from.description: Filesystem path from which each M1 document was loaded.
rules: loaded_from(m1_id) should remain available as long as that M1 document remains loaded.
This is load provenance, not an eternal home of identity.
Without k, if a new load comes from a path already represented here, previously loaded documents from that path are removed before the new one is loaded.
With k, previously loaded documents from that same path are preserved, so multiple loaded documents may coexist with the same loaded_from path.

priority.description: Ordered list of loaded M1 documents consulted after overlay.
rules: Overlay is not inside priority.
Any rearrangement of priority clears cache and source.
After rearrangement, cache and source must be recomputed by refresh or on demand.

selection_model.description: Visible aspect selection uses overlay first, then priority in order, subject to an entity-wide cutoff established by the selected entity tombstone aspect.
entity_tombstone_rules: First resolve tag:m1lattice.net,2026/aspect/tombstone using ordinary aspect selection.
If its selected value is null, lower-priority tombstone assertions are covered and no entity-wide cutoff is active.
If its selected value is an object whose 'entity' field is true, its source layer establishes the entity-wide cutoff.
Ordinary aspects may be selected only from layers above the cutoff.
Ordinary aspects in the tombstone's own layer and all lower-priority layers are covered.
An explicit entity contribution in a higher-priority layer reintroduces the entity, but does not expose aspects from at or below the cutoff.
If no higher-priority layer reintroduces the entity, the entity is absent from the visible entity surface.
Raw loaded documents and their tombstone claims remain preserved.

none_semantics.description: None is a legitimate aspect value.
none_semantics.meaning: This aspect on this entity is explicitly absent / deleted.
rules: None is a covering / masking value.
Once None is encountered during overlay / priority search, lookup stops.
Lower-priority M1 documents are not consulted further for that entity_id + aspect_id.

entity_tombstone_semantics.description: The conventional entity tombstone aspect expresses whole-entity absence in a layered view.
entity_tombstone_semantics.meaning: This entity is explicitly absent at this layer; its own-layer and lower-priority contributions are covered.
rules: The tombstone is represented as an aspect, not as a null entity entry.
It does not destroy the entity identifier or remove preserved source documents.
It does not invalidate links or other references to the identifier.
A higher-priority null value for the tombstone aspect covers the tombstone assertion itself.
A higher-priority explicit entity contribution may reintroduce the entity above the cutoff.
Entity tombstoning is distinct from save-time omission, which emits no mention of the entity and makes no absence assertion.

entity_aspect_access.name: get_aspect
entity_aspect_access.description: Return the selected visible value for aspect_id on the targeted entity.
rules: Consult overlay first, then priority in order.
May return None as a legitimate selected tombstone value.

entity_aspect_access.name: set_aspect
entity_aspect_access.description: Write directly to overlay for the targeted entity.
rules: aspect_data may be None.
Any call to set_aspect nullifies overlay.id.
set_aspect immediately updates cache for the targeted entity_id + aspect_id.
set_aspect immediately updates source for the targeted entity_id + aspect_id, with overlay as the effective source layer.

entity_aspect_access.name: tombstone_entity
entity_aspect_access.description: Write an active conventional entity tombstone aspect into overlay for the targeted entity.
rules: The emitted aspect id is tag:m1lattice.net,2026/aspect/tombstone.
The aspect value must contain entity=true.
Optional metadata may include reason, timestamp, agent, and additional keys.
This is equivalent to setting the conventional aspect directly, but provides an intention-revealing API.
The call nullifies the intended overlay m1.id and invalidates selected state for the entire targeted entity.

entity_aspect_access.name: has_entity_tombstone
entity_aspect_access.description: Return whether the selected conventional tombstone aspect is active for the targeted or specified entity.

entity_aspect_access.name: list_entities
entity_aspect_access.description: Return the total visible surface of entities known about.

entity_aspect_access.name: list_aspects
entity_aspect_access.description: Return visible aspects on the targeted entity, excluding aspects masked to None.

entity_aspect_access.name: list_aspects
entity_aspect_access.description: Return all selected aspects on the targeted entity, including aspects set to None and the selected entity tombstone aspect, but excluding ordinary aspects hidden at or below an active entity tombstone cutoff.

entity_aspect_access.name: has_entity
entity_aspect_access.description: Return whether the entity is present on the visible surface after applying any selected entity tombstone cutoff.

entity_aspect_access.name: has_aspect
entity_aspect_access.description: Return whether the targeted entity has a visible non-None value for the aspect.

entity_aspect_access.name: has_aspect
entity_aspect_access.description: Return whether the targeted entity has any presence for the aspect, including a None tombstone.

entity_aspect_access.name: get_surface
entity_aspect_access.description: Return the visible aspect surface of the targeted entity, excluding None-masked aspects.

entity_aspect_access.name: get_surface
entity_aspect_access.description: Return the full selected aspect surface of the targeted entity, including None tombstones and an active entity tombstone, but excluding ordinary aspects hidden at or below its cutoff.

sourcing.name: source_aspect
sourcing.description: Return the UUID of the M1 document that currently supplies the selected visible value for the targeted entity's aspect.

sourcing.name: loaded_from
sourcing.description: Return the filesystem path from which the M1 document was loaded.

m1_documents.name: list_m1
m1_documents.description: Return UUIDs of all loaded M1 documents.

m1_documents.name: has_m1
m1_documents.description: Return whether the M1 document is loaded.

m1_documents.name: drop_m1
rules: Calls clear_cache(m1_id).
Removes the document from M1.
Removes the path from M1_loaded_from.
Removes the document from priority.

m1_documents.name: drop_m1
rules: Clears M1.
Clears M1_loaded_from.
Clears priority.
Clears derived cache and source state.
Preserves overlay.

overlay.name: clear_overlay
overlay.description: Clear overlay entries and overlay metadata.

overlay.name: set_uuid
overlay.description: Manually set the intended emitted UUID for overlay by writing overlay_headers['id'].

overlay.name: stamp_creation
overlay.description: Set overlay.m1.created to the immediate current timestamp.
rules: This is used to mark the creation time of a document series.

overlay.name: set_headers
overlay.description: Directly specify additional or replacement header dictionary values to write into overlay.m1.
rules: This function writes into overlay_headers.
m1.timestamp remains runtime-controlled at write time.
The intended emitted UUID may be specified through the id header key.

overlay.name: weed_table
overlay.description: Inspect file-type entries in overlay_table and remove those whose filesystem paths no longer exist.

loading_saving.name: load_m1
loading_saving.description: Load an M1 file according to target-space and action flags.

loading_saving.name: save_m1
loading_saving.description: Save either overlay or snapshot according to target-space and action flags.

priority_ordering.name: list_priority
priority_ordering.description: Return M1 UUIDs in current search order.

priority_ordering.name: order
priority_ordering.description: Reorder priority. Any rearrangement clears cache and source.

priority_ordering.name: order
priority_ordering.description: Set explicit priority order by M1 UUID.

priority_ordering.name: order
priority_ordering.description: Reverse whatever ordering the requested type would otherwise imply.

cache_rebuild.name: clear_cache
cache_rebuild.description: Clear all derived selected cache values and per-aspect selected sourcing.

cache_rebuild.name: clear_cache
cache_rebuild.description: Clear cache/source entries for every entity_id + aspect_id where the given M1 document has any presence, including None.

cache_rebuild.name: clear_cache
cache_rebuild.description: Clear cache/source entries for every entity_id + aspect_id where overlay has any presence, including None.

cache_rebuild.name: refresh
cache_rebuild.description: Rebuild selected cache/source from overlay plus priority, and rebuild table_cache from overlay_table plus priority table contributions.

S.description: Save snapshot: the total visible surface of all known entities/aspects.

+.description: Amend save. If target file exists, load it, amend it with overlay/snapshot content, and write back.
rules: When amending, merge user-specified overlay_headers values on top of the existing m1 header.
If a header key is not specified in overlay_headers, do not erase the existing value.
The emitted result receives a fresh m1.id and a fresh m1.timestamp.

!.description: Overwrite save. Write directly over existing file.

-.description: Safe save. If target exists, raise and do not write.

t.description: Include table contributions in the save.

w.description: Perform table weeding before the save. May only be used if t is also present.

rules: O and S are mutually exclusive.
+, !, and - are mutually exclusive.
Snapshot save writes the currently visible value for every known entity/aspect using overlay first, then priority in order.
Snapshot save must also emit each selected active entity tombstone even when the tombstoned entity is absent from the ordinary visible entity surface.
Ordinary aspects hidden by an entity tombstone cutoff must not be emitted into a snapshot.
Without t, save does not include table contributions.
With t, overlay save includes overlay_table contributions, and snapshot save includes the total visible table surface.
w may only be used if t is also present.
With w, perform weed_table() before the save.
If a file already exists at the target path, load that file.
Update its M1 contents with what is present in the immediate snapshot.
Merge user-specified overlay_headers values on top of the existing m1 header.
If a header key is not specified in overlay_headers, do not erase the existing value.
The emitted result receives a fresh m1.id and a fresh m1.timestamp.
Write the amended result back to disk.
If a file already exists at the target path, raise an exception.
Do not overwrite the existing file.
Write the current snapshot directly over whatever is at the target path.

notes: O+ / O! / O- and S+ / S! / S- are the concrete save combinations.

k.description: Keep existing state during load: preserve overlay, and preserve previously loaded documents from the same path.

t.description: Include table contributions in the load.

w.description: Perform table weeding after the load. May only be used if t is also present.

O!.description: Replace overlay contents with the loaded document contents.

notes: None values are preserved as legitimate covering values.

rules: If overlay already contains any entity/aspect entries, raise and do not load.

S!.description: Replace the currently loaded world with the snapshot.
rules: Rebuild M1.
Rebuild priority.
Rebuild cache.
Rebuild source.
If k is absent: clear overlay.
If k is present: preserve overlay.

S+.description: Load the snapshot in the highest priority position while allowing everything else already loaded to remain.
rules: Add the snapshot to the loaded world.
Place it at highest priority.
Rebuild cache.
Rebuild source.

S-.description: Load the snapshot only if no other M1 data has been loaded.
rules: If other M1 data is already loaded, raise and do not load.

S!k.description: Replace the loaded-priority world from the snapshot while keeping overlay unchanged.

rules: O and S are mutually exclusive.
+, !, and - are mutually exclusive.
Without k, a successful load leaves at most one currently loaded document from the loaded path.
With k, multiple loaded documents may coexist from the same loaded path.
Without t, load does not include table contributions.
With t, table contributions are loaded through overlay_table / priority and invalidate affected table_cache identifiers.
w may only be used if t is also present.
With w, perform weed_table() after the load.

written_m1_minimum.description: Any written M1 document must meet this minimum.
notes: table remains valid and important in transport, but is not yet specified here in runtime terms.
This is a runtime minimum stated in the language of the current transport shape. It does not yet claim to replace the formal transport spec.

common_usage_pattern: Load the M1 file being edited.
Call set_aspect(...) to write edits into overlay.
Save back to the edited file using amend-style save.

rules: m1.id identifies a specific emitted transport artifact.
If content changes and is emitted again, the new emission must receive a new m1.id.
If content changes and is emitted again, the new emission must receive a new m1.timestamp.
m1.series_id may remain stable across multiple emissions in the same document series.
m1.created may remain stable as the creation time of that series.
The same filesystem path may therefore hold different transport UUIDs over time.

open_points: How this runtime minimum for written M1 documents should integrate with the formal transport spec remains to be settled.
Further table-runtime details beyond overlay_table, table_cache, t-flag behavior, and weed_table() may still deserve a dedicated document.
The API and state model for save-time omission, which is distinct from tombstoning, remains to be specified.
